macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. inflation worries intensify as consumer confidence weakens
items.summary: Recent commentary from White House economic officials emphasized stronger real wage growth, but surveys from Gallup and the University of Michigan showed sentiment and economic confidence falling to multi-year or record lows. The backdrop is persistent inflation pressure, with the April PCE price index up 3.8% year over year, the largest increase since May 2023.

items.title: Wall Street heads into jobs data with rate-cut hopes tied to inflation and labor market
items.summary: A market preview from Fidelity noted that U.S. stocks have been hovering near highs as investors focus on the upcoming jobs report and the path of Fed policy. The same report highlighted the April PCE inflation reading of 3.8% as a key reason rate-cut expectations remain sensitive to incoming data.

items.title: White House economic messaging pushes back on inflation fears
items.summary: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said real wages are rising and argued that recent consumer surveys overstate weakness in the economy. He also downplayed oil-supply concerns, saying inventories remain ample despite warnings that low stocks could pressure energy prices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and major altcoins rebound as BNB and HYPE lead a broad crypto recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin broke back above $74,000, while BNB and HYPE posted stronger gains, with BNB up 4.60% and HYPE up 5.96% in 24 hours. Binance ecosystem tokens also rallied sharply after reports and confirmation of Binance launching stock trading boosted market sentiment.

items.title: Crypto futures liquidations hit $204 million in 24 hours
items.summary: Across the market, crypto futures liquidations totaled $204 million in the past day, including $56.2 million in Bitcoin and $37.8 million in Ethereum. Long positions accounted for about $88 million of liquidations, while short positions made up roughly $116 million.

items.title: Bitcoin stabilizes above $73,000 as ETF outflows and regulatory developments shape sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $73,882, up 0.57% over 24 hours, after testing higher intraday levels near $74,089. Market commentary highlighted heavy U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF outflows, while also noting the CFTC's approval of regulated perpetual futures through Coinbase and Kalshi and SEC approval of options rule changes for several crypto-linked trusts and ETFs.

items.title: CME launches 24/7 crypto futures trading as Bitcoin trades just above $73,000
items.summary: Bitcoin was quoted at about $73,630 as CME's move to launch 24/7 crypto futures trading added a notable market-structure development. The report framed the move as a potentially important step for institutional crypto derivatives access.

items.title: Binance ecosystem tokens rally on stock-trading news
items.summary: BNB rose about 4.60% and several Binance-linked tokens gained sharply, including 'Binance Life' up more than 17%, ASTER up over 10%, and CAKE up over 9.2% in 24 hours. The move was tied to renewed enthusiasm after news that Binance was launching stock trading was confirmed.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google unveils Gemini 3.5 Flash, AI Mode at scale, and new agentic products
items.summary: At I/O 2026, Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash as the model behind its upgraded Search experience, said AI Mode has crossed a billion monthly users, and introduced Gemini Spark, a 24/7 personal AI agent inside Gmail. Google also announced Universal Cart for cross-merchant agentic checkout and Gemini Omni, a model that can turn any input into video.

items.title: Anthropic surpasses OpenAI in reported valuation and is said to be eyeing an IPO
items.summary: Anthropic is reported to have reached a $965 billion valuation, overtaking OpenAI’s reported $852 billion and becoming the most valuable AI startup. The report says Anthropic is targeting an IPO as soon as October.

items.title: Anthropic argues the U.S. frontier AI lead over China is narrowing
items.summary: In its paper "2028: Two scenarios for global AI leadership," Anthropic says U.S. AI is 12 to 24 months ahead of Chinese frontier models and argues 2026 is the decisive window. The paper highlights overseas compute access and large-scale distillation attacks as key ways Chinese labs can remain near the frontier.

items.title: OpenAI internal reasoning model proves a long-standing Erdős conjecture
items.summary: OpenAI announced that an internal reasoning model disproved a planar unit-distance conjecture posed by Paul Erdős in 1946, with Princeton mathematician Will Sawin refining the result. The development was described by Fields Medalist Tim Gowers as a milestone in AI mathematics.

items.title: New benchmark SOOHAK tests frontier AI math ability
items.summary: To probe advanced mathematical reasoning, 64 mathematicians released SOOHAK, a 439-task benchmark with 99 deliberately impossible problems. On a 79-task subset, only Gemini-3-Pro beat combined human coverage, reaching 61%.

items.title: Meta reportedly hires four more OpenAI researchers
items.summary: A report says Meta has hired Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren from OpenAI. The move underscores the ongoing competition among major AI labs for top research talent.

items.title: Apple reportedly preparing to open underlying AI technology to developers
items.summary: A report highlighted Apple’s plan to let developers access underlying AI technology, signaling a broader push to integrate its AI stack into third-party apps. The move could expand Apple’s role in on-device and platform-level AI development.

items.title: Industry trend: companies are pushing back on rising AI costs
items.summary: Tech Xplore reports that after an initial period of low pricing to drive adoption, many companies are now balking at rising AI bills. The story reflects a broader industry shift from growth-at-all-costs AI deployment to tighter scrutiny of unit economics.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
